files_modified: src/cli/populate-coderef.ts (or populate-coderef bin) — update --source-headers stamp format
All ~174 source files on feature/source-headers-dogfood — strip @semantic blocks + re-stamp with @coderef-semantic: 1.0.0
downstream_consumers: src/pipeline/semantic-header-parser.ts — will now detect @coderef-semantic: blocks (SEMANTIC_MARKER_RE match)
.coderef/graph.json — rebuilt with headerStatus: present on file-grain nodes

risks.title: Strip regex over-matches non-@semantic block comments
risks.mitigation: Review strip regex carefully; limit to @semantic blocks bounded by /** */ delimiters

risks.title: --source-headers format change breaks other consumers
risks.mitigation: Search codebase for @semantic consumers before changing format; update semantic-header-parser.ts if needed

risks.title: rag-index still fails on embedding (OpenAI key missing)
risks.mitigation: Success metric = chunks_skipped drops to 0 (header gate passes); embedding failures are pre-existing and non-blocking for this WO

decision_records.rationale: Fix --source-headers to emit @coderef-semantic: 1.0.0 full grammar. Minimal downstream impact — changes the stamper output to match the existing parser expectation. Options B (fix parser) and C (decouple) ruled out as higher risk / off-mission.
alternatives: B: fix semantic-header-parser to also accept @semantic
